summary: Recent updates to the image decoding system in Firefox focus on improving performance and resource management. By caching the specification hash on the URI (Uniform Resource Identifier), the browser can more efficiently retrieve image data, leading to faster loading times for images, especially on web pages with many visuals. Additionally, enhancements to track the parent process channel ensure smoother interactions between different components of the browser. These changes ultimately contribute to a more responsive user experience, particularly when dealing with image-heavy websites. **Improving how images are handled means visitors enjoy quicker access to visual content.**
